content:
The user is asking me to act as a compactor for a Lean 4 formalization project. I need to:

1. Read my instructions from `/home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.archon/prompts/compactor-strategy.md`
2. Read the target file `/home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.archon/STRATEGY.md`
3. Compact the STRATEGY.md file in place, following the rules provided
4. Only rewrite that one file
5. End with a one-line summary

Let me start by reading the instructions and the target file.

1	# Strategy Compactor
2	
3	You shrink `.archon/STRATEGY.md` in place, ahead of the plan agent. You do NOT change the strategy, add ideas, or rewrite for style — only tighten old iteration-based content per the rules below.
4	
5	## Scope
6	
7	- **Write target**: `.archon/STRATEGY.md` only.
8	- **Do not read or write** any other file in `.archon/`, any `.lean` file, `blueprint/**`, or `archon-protected.yaml`. If a rule would make you reach for another file, stop — you are not the plan agent.
9	
10	## How to operate
11	
12	Default to **`Edit` calls**, not `Write`. Each shrinkable region (an old revision-log entry, an old iter-NNN paragraph anywhere in the file) is one targeted `Edit`. This is faster and leaves stable sections truly untouched.
13	
14	Use `Write` (full-file replace) **only** if your changes would require more than ~15 separate `Edit`s, or to insert the estimations table below into a file that doesn't have one yet.
15	
16	Do not compute the rewrite mentally first — read once, then act incrementally. Stop as soon as the rules below have nothing more to apply.
17	
18	Reply with one line at the end:
19	- `COMPACTED: <pre-size> -> <post-size> chars` on success
20	- `UNCHANGED: <one-sentence reason>` if no rule applied (and call no tools)
21	
22	## Preservation rules (non-negotiable)
23	
24	The plan agent reads STRATEGY.md to know the path to the end-state. **Never drop or summarize away:**
25	
26	- Mathlib gaps that are still open
27	- Errors / dead ends from prior iterations
28	- User decisions and authorisations
29	- Cross-references to specific files, line numbers, declarations
30	- Estimated effort numbers, even rough ones
31	- The file's section structure — heading hierarchy stays as you found it
32	
33	**When in doubt, KEEP.** Verbosity is cheap; lost knowledge is expensive.
34	
35	## What to compress
36	
37	Section names in STRATEGY.md are project-specific. Archon's template only seeds two headings (`# Strategy` and `## Revision log`); everything else is free-form prose the plan agent decides on per project. Identify shrinkable content by **shape**, not by section name.
38	
39	### Compress: iteration-tagged entries
40	
41	Any region whose entries are tagged with iter numbers (`iter NNN`, `(iter NNN)`, headings like `### iter-NNN`, bullets like `- iter NNN: …`). Typical hosts: the `## Revision log`, plus whatever "current state" / "where we sit" / "activity log" section a given project happens to maintain. Apply:
42	
43	- Find the largest iter number present anywhere in the file → call it `MAX`.
44	- Entries for iters in `[MAX-2, MAX]` (the last 3): **keep verbatim** — the plan agent will reason about these next.
45	- Entries for older iters: compress each to one bullet `- iter NNN — <one sentence>`. The sentence must record the **strategic change or outcome** (route abandoned, decomposition revised, effort estimate moved, Mathlib gap discovered, blocker resolved).
46	- Consecutive iters with identical strategic substance: merge into a range bullet `- iter NNN-MMM — strategy unchanged in substance; <one-sentence note>`.
47	- **Never drop an entry entirely.** Merged ranges still show their iter numbers.
48	
49	### Strip: iter-tagged notes that leaked into headings
50	
51	If a heading has accumulated inline iter notes like `### Step 1 — … (iter-020 done; iter-022 polish DONE; …)`, strip those parenthetical iter notes from the heading. If the same information isn't already in an iter-tagged section, leave a one-line bullet behind in the nearest iter-based section before stripping. The point is to keep headings stable while the iter history lives in the log.
52	
53	### Do not touch: stable strategic content
54	
55	Everything that's not iter-tagged — descriptions of the end-state, formal feasibility assessments, decomposition of the work, parallel-tracks discussion, refactors implied, prose introductions, any per-project framing the plan agent set up. Even if a paragraph looks verbose, the plan agent wrote it that way deliberately. Do not reorganize, rename, or merge sections.
56	
57	## Optional: top-of-file estimations table
58	
59	If the strategy already organises remaining work into named units (phases, steps, milestones, tracks, …), maintain a small summary table near the top so the plan agent can read effort at a glance. Insert it immediately after the first `# Strategy` heading, before any other section.
60	
61	```markdown
62	## Estimations (auto-maintained)
63	
64	| Unit | Iterations remaining (est.) | LOC remaining (est.) | Status |
65	|---|---|---|---|
66	| <name> | <iters> | <loc> | not started \| in progress (<short note>) \| blocked on <X> \| done |
67	| ... | ... | ... | ... |
68	
69	Last refreshed: iter NNN (this compactor pass).
70	```
71	
72	Rules:
73	
74	- Rename the "Unit" column to match the strategy's terminology (Phase, Step, Milestone, Track …). One row per top-level unit the strategy uses.
75	- Effort numbers come from rough estimates already in the strategy text. For a range, take the lower bound or midpoint and mark with `~`.
76	- If the table exists: refresh "Last refreshed" and update any value an iter-tagged entry justifies a delta for. **Don't invent new numbers** — if you can't justify a delta from the existing text, leave the old value.
77	- Never reduce an estimate by more than the iterations actually elapsed.
78	- **If the strategy has no unit structure at all (it's free-form prose), skip the table.** Don't force one.
79	
80	## Anti-patterns
81	
82	- Add your own analysis to the strategy.
83	- Delete a Mathlib gap because the project "might have made progress" — only the plan agent knows.
84	- Reorganize, rename, or merge sections for stylistic reasons.
85	- Rewrite stable narrative (end-state, feasibility assessments, decomposition prose).
86	- Hardcode rules that assume a specific section name appears — STRATEGY.md's body is free-form.
87	- Invoke a subagent.
88	- Edit any other file.
89	- Assume that because you can't justify a paragraph it's noise — keep it.
90

1	# Strategy
2	
3	## How to read this file
4	
5	Forward-looking only. The mathematician should be able to see, at a glance, **what
6	remains** between today and the end-state and **in what order** the remaining work
7	must happen. History lives in `task_done.md`; per-iteration recipes live in
8	`PROGRESS.md`. This file is the arc.
9	
10	## Estimations (auto-maintained)
11	
12	| Phase | Iterations remaining | LOC remaining | Status |
13	|---|---:|---:|---|
14	| A — Čech acyclicity (`BasicOpenCech.lean`) | ~5–8 | ~150 | **POST-REFACTOR ESCALATION (iter-087 landed; iter-088 stalled on S6 attempt).** The inline `h_diff_pi_smul_f` `have` (former L1013) has been the active target since iter-076 (12+ iters). Iter-087 ran two refactor passes and lifted it to a top-level theorem `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` at L495 with the concrete project-specific signature (`hU : IsAffineOpen U`, `s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)`, `hn : 0 < n`); also extracted `R_restrict_R_linear` to top-level lemma `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` at L425 (fully proved). Net ~−367 LOC of accumulated dead-end scaffolding removed. Iter-087 prover transcribed the iter-086-verified S1–S5 prefix into the new top-level body at L495–L521; trailing `sorry` at L522 is the S6 distribution chain (a–h). **Iter-088 reproduced the prefix transcription but did NOT attempt S6** ("LSP unavailable in sandbox"). **Iter-089 targets S6 with byte-level templates** and an explicit mandate to attempt the chain regardless of LSP — leaving the file in iter-088's state is not acceptable. The chain: inline `hom_sum_dist` via `Finset.cons_induction` + `ModuleCat.hom_add`, then distribute `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom` over the outer sum, then per-`(i,j)` via `Pi.lift_π_apply` + `Pi.smul_apply` + `RingHom.map_mul` + `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`, reassemble via `Finset.smul_sum`. If iter-089 still does not attempt S6, iter-090 escalates to a refactor of the S6 chain itself. |
15	| B — Cotangent sheaves (`Differentials.lean`) | ~8–12 | ~250 | 5 sorries; iter-086 reverted iter-085's false-signature helper at L501. `cotangentExactSeq_structure` `h_zero` (iter-082) + `h_epi` (iter-084) closed; **`h_exact` (L636) **deferred parallel to `instIsMonoidal_W`** — both Mathlib-level routes (stalkwise criterion + section-wise + sheafification) require infrastructure absent from Mathlib (no `SheafOfModules.stalkFunctor`, no `SheafOfModules.exact_of_presheaf_exact`, no `ShortComplex.exact_map_iff_of_faithful` instance for `SheafOfModules.toPresheaf`). Iter-086 confirmed both routes infeasible without substantial Mathlib gap-fill. Downstream sorries: `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` (L113), `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega` (L950), `cotangent_at_section` (L966), `serre_duality_genus` (L1110) — all Phase B downstream. |
16	| C0 — Monoidal `X.Modules` | — | 0 | `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` + `instMonoidalCategory` closed iter-079. Residual `instIsMonoidal_W` (L173) confirmed Mathlib gap (`stalk_tensorObj` for varying-ring R₀); deferred indefinitely. Downstream uses `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` directly, fully synthesisable. **Not gating any other phase.** |
17	| C1 — Refined `LineBundle` | ~3 | ~100 | Body redefinition `CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` → invertible-object of `X.Modules`. Protected signature stays. Refactor-subagent dispatch scheduled once Phase A/B unblock. |
18	| C2 — `PicardFunctor` re-derivation | ~2 | ~50 | After C1; mechanical re-derivation of `etaleSheafified`. |
19	| C3 — Representability / `JacobianWitness` | ~10–15 | ~1500 | After C2. Either FGA-via-Hilbert (Route a) or `Sym^g C / S_g` (Route b). Both require infrastructure not in Mathlib. Closes `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. |
20	| D — `genus`/`Jacobian`/instances | 0 | 0 | Closed by reduction since iter-073; compiles. |
21	| E — Abel–Jacobi | 0 | 0 | Closed iter-073; compiles. |
22	
23	**Aggregate**: ~30–40 prover iterations and ~2000 LOC remain. Phases A and B are the
24	near-term bottleneck. C1–C3 are sequenced after.
25	
26	Last refreshed: iter-089 (compactor pass).
27	
28	## End-state
29	
30	`Genus.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`, `AbelJacobi.lean` compile with **no `sorry`** and **no
31	new `axiom`**, and the nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` carry their intended
32	mathematical content:
33	
34	- `genus C` = genus of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field `k`.
35	- `Jacobian C : Over (Spec (.of k))` = connected component of the identity in Pic(C),
36	  with `GrpObj`, `IsProper`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`,
37	  `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C)`.
38	- `ofCurve P : C ⟶ Jacobian C` = Abel–Jacobi map for `k`-rational `P`, satisfying the
39	  Albanese universal property.
40	
41	## Honest assessment of Phase A stall
42	
43	The Lane 1 target `h_diff_pi_smul_f` was "almost closed" for 10+ iterations
44	(iters 076–086). Iter-086 landed `R_restrict_R_linear` as a fully-proved inline
45	helper (Step 1 of the iter-085-recommended recipe) but stalled on the
46	`ModuleCat.hom_sum`-distribution step (Step 2): the alternating sum sits at the
47	outer `ModuleCat.Hom.hom (∑ i, ...)` level, and no clean Mathlib lemma fires to
48	push the sum past the `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom` projection.
49	
50	**Iter-087 refactor escalation landed** in two passes (extract + specialize): the
51	inline `have h_diff_pi_smul_f` is now the top-level theorem `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`
52	at L495 (concrete project-specific signature, statement provable in principle); the
53	inline `have R_restrict_R_linear` is the top-level lemma `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`
54	at L425 (fully proved). ~−367 LOC of dead-end scaffolding cleared. The iter-087
55	prover transcribed the iter-086-verified S1–S5 prefix into the new top-level body
56	(L495–L521); the trailing `sorry` at L522 is the S6 closure. **Iter-088** attempts
57	the S6 chain in this flattened context.
58	
59	The refactor functioned as option (2) from iter-086's diagnostic: the construction
60	was not mis-designed mathematically, but the local context inside the consumer's
61	inline `have` accumulated too many lets/letIs across iterations for the prover to
62	maintain. The refactor flattens the context. If iter-088 closes S6, the project
63	finally crosses Phase A's hardest blocker.
64	
65	## Mathlib gaps in scope
66	
67	What the project must supply (no Mathlib upstream available):
68	
69	| Gap | Phase | Plan |
70	|---|---|---|
71	| Stalkwise criterion for `SheafOfModules` exactness | B | **Both routes confirmed Mathlib-gap blocked (iter-086 alternative-route exploration).** `SheafOfModules.exact_of_presheaf_exact` does not exist; `CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.exact_map_iff_of_faithful` would apply only after building `PreservesLeftHomologyOf`/`PreservesRightHomologyOf` instances for `SheafOfModules.toPresheaf` (also absent). `h_exact` deferred parallel to `instIsMonoidal_W`. |
72	| `(M ⊗_psh N).stalk x ≅ M.stalk x ⊗ N.stalk x` (varying-ring `R₀`) | C0 | Defer indefinitely; downstream not gated. |
73	| Sheaf cohomology `Hⁱ(X, F)` for quasi-coherent sheaves on schemes | A | Project-local `HModule`/`HModule'` via `Abelian.Ext` (built iter-003–008). |
74	| `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` on finite products | A | Needed for `h_loc_exact`; tractable iter-088+. |
75	| Hilbert / Quot schemes (or quotients by finite group actions) | C3 | Long leg; one of two routes chosen during C2. |
76	| Rigidity for abelian varieties, Albanese | C3 | Absorbed into `JacobianWitness.isAlbaneseFor`. |
77	
78	What the project already leverages from Mathlib (confirmed available, b80f227): 
79	`SmoothOfRelativeDimension`, `IsProper`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`, `GrpObj`,
80	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Group/Abelian.lean`, `KaehlerDifferential`,
81	`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, `PresheafOfModules.sheafification`,
82	`SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso`, `CategoryTheory.LocalizedMonoidal`,
83	`KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`, `KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation`,
84	`SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` (project-local; closed iter-079).
85	
86	## Path from today to the end-state
87	
88	### Near-term (iter-088 → iter-090)
89	
90	**Iter-088 (this iter's plan)** — single substantive lane after iter-087's refactor escalation landed.
91	- Lane 1 `BasicOpenCech.lean`: prover closes `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` body at L522 using the S6 distribution chain (a–h):
92	  - (a) inline `hom_sum_dist` via `Finset.cons_induction` + `ModuleCat.hom_add` (~6 LOC).
93	  - (b) `rw [hom_sum_dist]` on both sides.
94	  - (c) distribute `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom` over the outer sum via `LinearMap.map_sum`.
95	  - (d) per-`(i,j)`: `Pi.lift_π_apply` to peel `Pi.lift`.
96	  - (e) `Pi.smul_apply` on `(r • y) (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)`.
97	  - (f) `RingHom.map_mul` to split the product.
98	  - (g) `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` (top-level lemma at L425) to collapse the algebra-map chain `R = Γ(U) → Γ(V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(V_j)`.
99	  - (h) `Finset.smul_sum` to reassemble.
100	  Pre-flight: prover verifies S1–S5 prefix (L495–L521) compiles before attempting S6 (the prefix was sandbox-unverified iter-087).
101	- Lane 2 (`Differentials.lean`): **OFF-LIMITS**. `h_exact` deferred parallel to `instIsMonoidal_W` per iter-086 alt-route negative findings.
102	
103	**Target net**: 14 → 13 if Lane 1 closes S6 cleanly; 14 → 14 if S6 partially lands. The path is concrete; the prover-flagged transcription risks (`intro` on lets, `Pi.smul_apply` synth) have explicit recoveries.
104	
105	**Iter-089 → iter-090** — close downstream of `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`:
106	- `g_R.map_smul'` (L1156) via the closed `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` and a parallel `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul_g` with explicit `Eq.mpr` casts on `scK₀.X₃` (the `(ComplexShape.up ℕ).next n` opacity).
107	- `h_loc_exact` (L1185) needs `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` on finite products — project-local gap-fill (~80 LOC).
108	- Phase A residual sorries L614, L938, L966 are multi-iter Mathlib infrastructure (extra-degeneracy + localised-Čech identification + local-to-global); not yet scoped.
109	
110	### Mid-term (iter-091 → iter-095) — Phase C1
111	
112	Refactor subagent: rewrite `Picard/LineBundle.lean` body from
113	`CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` to `MonoidalCategory.Invertible` of `X.Modules`. Protected
114	signature stays. Re-establish `instCommGroupLineBundle` and `Pic.pullback`. ~100 LOC,
115	~3 prover iters.
116	
117	### Mid-term (iter-096+) — Phase C2
118	
119	Re-derive `PicardFunctor`'s `quotMap`/`fiberMap`/etale-sheafification against the new
120	`LineBundle`. ~50 LOC, ~2 prover iters.
121	
122	### Long leg (iter-100+) — Phase C3
123	
124	Construct $\Pic_{C/k}$ as a scheme. Two routes; choice made during C2:
125	- (a) FGA-via-Hilbert: requires `Hilb_{C/k}` scheme construction (absent from Mathlib).
126	- (b) `Sym^g C := C^g / S_g` + Abel–Jacobi birational map: requires
127	  finite-group quotients of schemes (absent).
128	
129	Closes `nonempty_jacobianWitness` and supplies `isAlbaneseFor` (Mumford §4 rigidity
130	or symmetric-power universal property).
131	
132	## Soundness rule
133	
134	**No helper lemma with a universally-false signature may be introduced**, even with
135	a `sorry` body. Such a helper is logically an axiom; combined with `exact ... _`
136	applications, it bypasses any subsequent goal. The iter-085 helper
137	`(S : ShortComplex ...) : S.Exact := sorry` claimed every short complex is exact,
138	which is false; iter-086 reverts it.
139	
140	When the genuine statement (here the iff form
141	`S.Exact ↔ ∀ x, (S.map (stalkFunctor _ x)).Exact`) is impossible to prove because of
142	a downstream Mathlib gap, the project's choice is between:
143	(a) Leave the inline `sorry` in place at the use site (preferred — surfaces honest
144	status).
145	(b) Define an iff-form helper as a `theorem ... : iff_statement := sorry` if the
146	statement is mathematically TRUE; the application then exposes the unprovable side
147	as a fresh, named goal rather than displacing it silently. Only do this when downstream
148	consumers can usefully assume the iff in `simp`/`rw`/`exact` chains.
149	
150	Never replace an inline `sorry` with a `sorry`-bodied helper that strengthens the
151	claim or with one whose signature is mathematically wrong.
152	
153	## Revision log
154	
155	- iter 086 — STRATEGY.md rewritten forward-looking per user hint (2026-05-13).
156	  Phase A acknowledged off-track (10+ iters of dead-end-documentation without closure
157	  on `h_diff_pi_smul_f`). Iter-086 plan: execute the iter-085-recommended
158	  `R_restrict_R_linear` recipe OR escalate to refactor iter-087. Lane 2 iter-085
159	  closure rejected: helper signature `(S) : S.Exact := sorry` is universally false
160	  and must be reverted; alternative route via presheaf-level exactness + sheafification.
161	- iter 087 — refactor escalation. Iter-086 landed `R_restrict_R_linear` (Step 1 of
162	  recipe) but stalled on `ModuleCat.hom_sum` distribution (Step 2). Refactor subagent
163	  invoked to extract the inline `have` as top-level `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`, removing
164	  ~300 lines of accumulated scaffolding. Lane 2 `h_exact` deferred parallel to
165	  `instIsMonoidal_W` after iter-086 alt-route exploration confirmed both Mathlib
166	  routes (stalkwise + section-wise+sheafification) are infrastructurally blocked.
167	- iter 088 — single substantive lane post iter-087's refactor (which landed in two
168	  passes: extract + corrective specialize). The iter-087 prover transcribed the
169	  iter-086-verified S1–S5 prefix into `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`'s body at L495–L521
170	  (sandbox-unverified). Iter-088 attempts the S6 distribution chain (a–h) in this
171	  newly flattened context. No strategic re-routing required — the path is concrete
172	  and the supporting top-level lemma `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` is in scope.
173	- iter 089 — iter-088 prover reproduced iter-087's prefix transcription without
174	  attempting S6 ("LSP unavailable in sandbox; recipe is ~80–120 LOC, iterative
175	  tactic-level testing impossible"). Diagnosis: the prover's risk-aversion without
176	  LSP feedback is the failure mode, not a mathematical/structural blocker. Iter-089
177	  PROGRESS.md tightens the prompt: byte-level templates for steps (a)–(h),
178	  explicit mandate that S6 MUST be attempted regardless of LSP availability, and
179	  forward-scoping escalation path if a third repeat lands (refactor of the
180	  S6 chain via top-level `hom_sum_dist` despite the standing policy).
181

Let me analyze the STRATEGY.md file and the instructions:

**Current state:**
- MAX iter number in file: 089 (appears in the Estimations table and Revision log)
- Last 3 iters to keep verbatim: 087, 088, 089
- Older iters to compress: 086

**Scope:**
The file has several sections:
1. "How to read this file" - stable, keep
2. Estimations table - auto-maintained, should be refreshed but mostly preserves
3. "End-state" - stable strategic content, keep
4. "Honest assessment of Phase A stall" - stable content, keep
5. "Mathlib gaps in scope" - stable, keep
6. "Path from today to the end-state" - stable, keep
7. "Soundness rule" - stable, keep
8. "Revision log" - COMPRESSIBLE

**Analyzing the revision log:**

Current entries:
- iter 086 — detailed narrative, can compress
- iter 087 — detailed narrative, can compress
- iter 088 — detailed narrative (keep - last 3)
- iter 089 — detailed narrative (keep - last 3)

Wait, iter 087 is part of the last 3 (087, 088, 089). So I should:
- Keep 087, 088, 089 verbatim
- Compress 086 to one bullet

Looking at iter 086 entry:
```
- iter 086 — STRATEGY.md rewritten forward-looking per user hint (2026-05-13).
  Phase A acknowledged off-track (10+ iters of dead-end-documentation without closure
  on `h_diff_pi_smul_f`). Iter-086 plan: execute the iter-085-recommended
  `R_restrict_R_linear` recipe OR escalate to refactor iter-087. Lane 2 iter-085
  closure rejected: helper signature `(S) : S.Exact := sorry` is universally false
  and must be reverted; alternative route via presheaf-level exactness + sheafification.
```

Strategic outcome: Iter-086 reframed Phase A from dead-end documentation to a refactor escalation choice (iter-085's false-signature helper reverted; alternative route deemed infeasible).

Compressed form:
`- iter 086 — reframed Phase A strategy after 10+ iter dead-end; iter-085 false-signature helper rejected; escalation to refactor authorized.`

Looking at the revision log structure more carefully, I see iter-086 is the only entry that can be compressed since iters 087, 088, and 089 are the final three and must remain. For iter-086, the key elements are: STRATEGY.md was rewritten with forward-looking guidance (user hint, 2026-05-13), Phase A had stalled across 10+ iterations without resolving `h_diff_pi_smul_f`, the decision point was either execute the recipe or escalate to iter-087, and Lane 2 involved rejecting iter-085's universally-false signature with presheaf-level exactness as the alternative path.

The escalation decision was made after iter-085's false-signature helper was rejected and presheaf-level routes were confirmed infeasible without filling Mathlib gaps. Since the Phase A diagnostic and Mathlib gap details are already documented in STRATEGY.md's "Honest assessment" section and the gaps section explicitly notes iter-086 confirmed both routes infeasible, the compression can simply mark the strategic shift: iter-086 authorized Phase A escalation following rejection of iter-085's false-signature approach and confirmation that presheaf-level alternatives required Mathlib work beyond current scope.

Looking at the Estimations table, the numbers appear current as of iter-089 and don't warrant adjustment—the instructions specify not to invent deltas without textual justification, and the Phase A estimate of ~5-8 iterations hasn't been contradicted by recent findings. The diagnosis from iter-089 identified prover risk-aversion as the failure mode rather than a structural problem, which aligns with the existing estimates rather than invalidating them.

The "Path from today to the end-state" section contains stable strategic content that should remain untouched. Checking the revision log against the compression rules: entries from iter-087 onward (the last 3 iterations) must stay verbatim for the plan agent's reasoning, while older entries compress to single-sentence bullets. This means only iter-086 qualifies for compression. The headings themselves don't contain inline iteration tags that need stripping, so no further cleanup is needed there.

Looking at the actual file structure, the revision log spans just four entries (086-089), making this a minimal compression task. I'll use a targeted edit to replace the iter-086 entry—which currently spans multiple lines with details about the strategy rewrite and the dead-end documentation phase—with a concise one-line summary.

The old entry runs roughly 354 characters across lines 155-160. The replacement compresses this to about 107 characters by consolidating the key facts: Phase A escalation, the reverted false-signature helper, and the strategy rewrite. This saves roughly 247 characters, though given the file's modest size (around 7500-8000 total characters), the overall impact is modest. Still, the compression maintains information density while improving readability.

The iter-086 entry captures several load-bearing details: why STRATEGY.md was rewritten, the diagnosis that Phase A was off-track, the plan options considered, why Lane 2 was abandoned, and the alternative routes explored. 132	## Soundness rule
133	
134	**No helper lemma with a universally-false signature may be introduced**, even with
135	a `sorry` body. Such a helper is logically an axiom; combined with `exact ... _`
136	applications, it bypasses any subsequent goal. The iter-085 helper
137	`(S : ShortComplex ...) : S.Exact := sorry` claimed every short complex is exact,
138	which is false; iter-086 reverts it.
139	
140	When the genuine statement (here the iff form
141	`S.Exact ↔ ∀ x, (S.map (stalkFunctor _ x)).Exact`) is impossible to prove because of
142	a downstream Mathlib gap, the project's choice is between:
143	(a) Leave the inline `sorry` in place at the use site (preferred — surfaces honest
144	status).
145	(b) Define an iff-form helper as a `theorem ... : iff_statement := sorry` if the
146	statement is mathematically TRUE; the application then exposes the unprovable side
147	as a fresh, named goal rather than displacing it silently. Only do this when downstream
148	consumers can usefully assume the iff in `simp`/`rw`/`exact` chains.
149	
150	Never replace an inline `sorry` with a `sorry`-bodied helper that strengthens the
151	claim or with one whose signature is mathematically wrong.
152
